acceptance_targets: [CH14_BRIEFING_ARCHITECTURE] Pass if the revised planning sequence is at most 350 lines and the operational information emerges primarily through character disagreement, telepathic intrusion, and physical business rather than enumerated assignments. Fail if the chapter still reads as a briefing document with domestic breaks between operational paragraphs.
[R011] Ch14 does not independently re-prove Yor's cooking incompetence through comic food description.
[R012] Ch14 does not re-enumerate that Anya has never told anyone about telepathy.
[LW06-001] A cold reader can identify both the location and who "he" is within the first two sentences of chapter 14 without rereading.
[LW06-003] Before Yor says she has something, chapter 14 has already added new leverage instead of restating the Crane, mole, and summit package the reader just absorbed in chapter 13.
[LW06-004] A reader can explain in one sentence why Anya could not realistically go to the Forgers earlier and why that blocker outweighed giving them more time before the summit.

[LW07-001] After reading chapters 13 and 14 consecutively, the reader gets no more than one reminder that Damian knows Anya's identity before the scene delivers a materially new consequence of that knowledge.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised planning sequence is at most 350 lines and the operational information emerges primarily through character disagreement, telepathic intrusion, and physical business rather than enumerated assignments. Fail if the chapter still reads as a briefing document with domestic breaks between operational paragraphs.

evidence.excerpt: 7474: Loid let the silence sit for a breath, then another, then shifted back to the table because that was what he did when the wall was already on top of them.

7964: Loid rewrote the assignments on the whiteboard. "Yor, foyer junction to south perimeter. Me, east corridor relay and secondary technical station. Damian, West Gallery and Voss during seating. Anya, East Gallery press row."
7980: "Then she calls it," he said. "Nothing heroic."

7964: Loid rewrote the assignments on the whiteboard. "Yor, foyer junction to south perimeter. Me, east corridor relay and secondary technical station. Damian, West Gallery and Voss during seating. Anya, East Gallery press row."

findings.problem: The family planning session runs 500+ lines of characters explaining operational details to each other: relay timing, junction routes, assignment boards, badge logistics, and summit layout. While the domestic texture (curry, Bond, wine) provides some embodied counterweight, the chapter's core mode is expository briefing. The style bible's own forbidden drift pattern 'briefing-itis' describes exactly this failure: 'scenes where characters explain plot to each other. Information should emerge through action, observation, and telepathic fragments -- not expository dialogue.' The chapter reads as an operational planning document with domestic decoration rather than a dramatic scene with operational content.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the planning sequence at snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7474-7964. Cut or compress the explicit operational walk-throughs (relay timing, junction routes, badge logistics) by at least 40%. Replace procedural exposition with embodied conflict: Loid and Damian's first face-to-face meeting should carry more tension about trust, authority, and who gets to decide what Anya does; Yor's break from the Garden should generate more friction about what she will and won't do; the tactical disagreements should be dramatized through character collision rather than stated and resolved. Keep the whiteboard map as a physical anchor but let the reader infer the plan from what the characters argue about rather than what they enumerate.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch14 does not independently re-prove Yor's cooking incompetence through comic food description.
findings.evidence: Ch14 L7558: 'rice tasted like sesame and old metal'; Ch14 L7560: 'Yor apologized once for overcooking the vegetables'; established baseline in Ch1 L335, Ch6 L3696, Ch10 L5588
findings.problem: Yor's bad cooking is re-proved in Ch14 with the same template (food described in comic-failure terms, Yor apologizes). This is the 5th independent proof of the same baseline. Ch14's cooking failure adds no new leverage or consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the cooking-failure details at L7558-7560. The planning dinner scene can reference food being served without re-proving the gag.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch14 does not re-enumerate that Anya has never told anyone about telepathy.
findings.evidence: Ch14 L7378: 'She did not tell them about telepathy...every useful thread she'd pulled had started with a thought she'd stolen'; established in Ch1 L17 (can't turn it off), Ch3 L850 (information nobody chose to give), Ch4 L2578 (its own rot), Ch16 L8878 (pivotal disclosure scene)
findings.problem: Anya's 'can't tell anyone about telepathy' is enumerated at Ch14 L7378 as the 4th re-statement of this baseline. The reader has known since Ch1 that Anya keeps her telepathy secret. Ch16 L8878 is the pivotal disclosure that earns its enumeration. Ch14's re-statement is the most expendable because it re-proves what the reader already knows without adding new consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: At L7378, cut the enumeration of who hasn't been told. Trust the reader to carry the secret-keeping premise from Ch1. If telepathy's relevance to the planning scene needs flagging, do it through action (Anya reading thoughts) rather than re-stating the concealment.

findings.acceptance_test: A cold reader can identify both the location and who "he" is within the first two sentences of chapter 14 without rereading.

evidence.excerpt: 7224: Three days after David Crane died at the Kreuzfeld border checkpoint, Anya stood outside the apartment door with the key in her hand and thought, not for the first time, that he had no business being in this hallway on a Tuesday night. He was there anyway. The apartment smelled of sesame oil and sugar catching at the bottom of a pan. Sylvia's voice: *We lost Crane at the checkpoint.* The checkpoint lights. The gap in the coverage pattern Anya had helped cut open with a lie.

findings.problem: Chapter 14 opens with two masculine pronouns before the scene anchors who "he" is. Coming straight off Damian's POV and repeated Crane references, a reader has to stop and test Damian versus Loid versus Crane before the apartment context settles. That kind of pause is accidental boundary confusion, not productive suspense.
findings.rewrite_direction: Name Loid directly, or reframe the opening so the Forger-apartment context lands before any pronoun-only reference. If the intent is that Loid now feels newly strange as both father and handler, make that strangeness explicit instead of suspending antecedent clarity.

findings.acceptance_test: Before Yor says she has something, chapter 14 has already added new leverage instead of restating the Crane, mole, and summit package the reader just absorbed in chapter 13.

evidence.excerpt: 6827: "I filed the east-sector picture wrong," she said. "I made it smaller than it was. I said it was ego and old men talking war after dessert. I pushed the customs piece and the freight piece down the stack. WISE thinned the eastern watch after that. They opened substitute lanes through crossings they thought were quieter." Her throat felt scraped raw. "Crane took one."
6971: "What did you have when you wrote this?" Sylvia asked.
7290: "Pieces. Border breach. Dead courier. Noise in the procurement channels." She forced herself to meet his eyes. "Not the whole shape. Not from me."

findings.problem: Chapter 13 already runs Anya through two consequence-filter scenes: Damian refuses a clean self-indictment, then Sylvia forces the distinction between known and guessed facts. Chapter 14 opens by making Loid run a third version of the same beat, so the reader sits through another partial confession and another insistence on the solid part before Yor's envelope finally changes the state of play. Read back-to-back, the first movement of chapter 14 feels like another debrief rather than a new engine.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress Loid's cross-examination or make it produce materially new information immediately. Move Yor's envelope into the scene sooner, or let Loid's questioning expose a fact the reader does not already possess from chapter 13.

findings.acceptance_test: A reader can explain in one sentence why Anya could not realistically go to the Forgers earlier and why that blocker outweighed giving them more time before the summit.

evidence.excerpt: 6729: "We need to meet," Anya said. "Not the usual place."

7157: Anya went back to the terminal and kept working until the words on the screen turned liquid around the edges. At 16:40 she sent Sylvia the first clean reconstruction: the lie she filed, the downgrade it triggered, the coverage cuts that followed, the specific window Crane inherited, the points where uncertainty still started. She left the building after dark with the route map burned behind her eyes and the smell of dead coffee in her coat.

7224: Three days after David Crane died at the Kreuzfeld border checkpoint, Anya stood outside the apartment door with the key in her hand and thought, not for the first time, that he had no business being in this hallway on a Tuesday night. He was there anyway. The apartment smelled of sesame oil and sugar catching at the bottom of a pan. Sylvia's voice: *We lost Crane at the checkpoint.* The checkpoint lights. The gap in the coverage pattern Anya had helped cut open with a lie.

7312: "I kept thinking I could get one more clean piece," Anya said. "One thing nobody could argue with. One thing that would make it look less like panic and more like a case file. I kept thinking if I came to you with enough in my hand I could do this right, and every time I had something new it still wasn't enough."

findings.problem: After Crane dies, Anya moves immediately to meet Damian and spends the same day mapping damage with Sylvia, but the crucial step of warning Loid and Yor is deferred off-page for three full days until the summit is essentially upon them. The text acknowledges that she should have come sooner, yet the offered reason, wanting one more clean piece, feels thinner than the operational urgency chapter 13 establishes. On a cluster read, the delay feels arranged to hold the family-alliance chapter in place rather than emerging from believable pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Either add a concrete blocker that makes those three days impossible or dangerous, such as surveillance, WISE lockdown, or a specific reason home contact would burn the remaining line, or shorten the gap so the family alliance activates much closer to the chapter-13 collapse.

findings.acceptance_test: After reading chapters 13 and 14 consecutively, the reader gets no more than one reminder that Damian knows Anya's identity before the scene delivers a materially new consequence of that knowledge.
findings.evidence: Chapter 13 already makes Damian's recognition explicit in the photo scene and states that he will keep working with Anya without telling her yet (snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7185, snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7217). Chapter 14 then spends fresh beats on him suppressing 'Eden' and swapping in safer thoughts when he enters the Forger apartment (snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7748, snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7824).

evidence.excerpt: 7185: He was not looking for proof. He already had that. Becky's drunken sentence at the gala had closed the last inch of distance. The photo made the certainty worse, not clearer.
7217: Then he went back to the bed, slid the class photo under his mother's note, and shut the lid. He would keep working with her. He would not hand her the photo. Not yet.

7748: He stepped into the kitchen damp from the rain, coat dark at the shoulders, hair flattened at the temples. He looked like he had been sleeping badly in clean shirts. When his eyes found Anya, the next thought came too fast and too polished.
7824: The swerve was so clean Anya almost missed it. But she saw the glance at the hall. She heard the swap. Harmless thoughts lifted into place where a riskier one had almost gone.

findings.problem: Read back-to-back, chapter 14 re-explains a plot fact the reader already has in full: Damian knows exactly who Anya is and is managing what she can catch. Because chapter 13 has already converted that from suspicion into certainty, the new concealment beats in chapter 14 play less like fresh tension and more like repeated dramatic irony.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep one reminder beat when Damian enters the apartment, then spend the rest of his page time on a new consequence of that knowledge: a sharper tactical choice, a changed emotional reaction to the Forger household, or a concrete risk he is now actively managing.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

Audit every revised passage for hedge words ('something,' 'somehow,' 'seemed to,' 'a kind of,' 'almost'). Replace with specifics wherever the referent can be identified. Intentional vagueness is permitted only when a character is genuinely uncertain or a telepathic signal is genuinely fragmentary. The default must be precision, not approximation.

In any revised dialogue exchange of three or more volleys, ensure at least one moment of rhythmic imbalance: a line that lands wrong, a response at the wrong register, a silence where speech was expected, or a sentence the speaker visibly regrets starting. Do not produce perfectly weighted stichomythia where every line advances the theme at the same pace and length.

Once a chapter's dramatic climax has landed, close within 10-15 lines. Do not extend into narratorial meditation, moral processing, or enumeration of what the climax means. End on a physical detail, an unresolved image, or a single unprocessed sensation. The reader carries the meaning; the chapter's job is to leave them in the collision, not to summarize it.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's premise blends comedy, espionage action, and genuine emotional stakes. The creative risk is in honoring all three simultaneously without letting any one mode cannibalize the others. Do not soften the violence because the novel is funny. Do not suppress the comedy because the stakes are real. Do not retreat from Anya's moral complicity (she reads minds without consent and uses it to manipulate) because she is the protagonist and we like her. The hardest scenes to get right are the ones where comedy and pain coexist — a fight that is also funny, a confession that is also absurd, a family dinner where love and deception are the same meal. Lean into those moments rather than resolving them into one register. The novel's best material lives in the dissonance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When violence, fear, grief, or moral compromise demands scene presence, render it with specificity. A bruise should have a color. A death should have weight. A lie should have a cost the liar feels in their body. The exception is Anya's telepathic restraint — she sometimes cannot bear to fully articulate what she's reading in someone's mind, and this selective omission is a character choice, not authorial squeamishness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in casual speech. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific register choices (Sylvia's professional diction, Damian's diplomatic mode). In contemporary casual scenes, an unjustified full-form line should be flagged and checked.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Alternate between dialogue-heavy scenes and interiority-heavy passages. Vary scene count: two-scene chapters (11, 16, 17 — compressed force) against multi-scene chapters (8, 15 — breadth and momentum). Shift between comic dominant and dramatic dominant across the middle of the book, with the comic register maintaining presence even in dramatic chapters through Anya's voice and the Forger family's inherent absurdity. The tonal shift at Chapter 13 is a deliberate gear change, not a gradual dimming — comedy should be fully alive in Chapter 12 and markedly reduced (not absent) in Chapter 13. The Chapter 13 POV shift to Damian is a structural event: the reader's relationship to information changes. The final act (14-17) maintains Anya's voice and the family's warmth as comic undertone, but the dominant mode is drama with consequences. Chapter 17's dinner scene reintroduces comedy through a register the novel has not previously accessed — Damian's self-conscious thought management around a known telepath — which only works because it follows sixteen chapters of oblivious thought-reading.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary and accessible, not literary-precious. The prose should have energy and forward drive. Humor comes from observation, character logic, and the gap between surface and interior — not from clever narration or authorial commentary. Let the funny things be funny without signposting them. Let the sad things sit without interpretation. The prose should be invisible when it needs to be and vivid when the scene demands it, but never decorative for its own sake.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Narrator-as-comedian: the prose commenting on its own comedy, winking at the reader, or admiring a character's wit. The humor lives in the situation, not in the telling.

Telepathy-as-omniscience: Anya's mind-reading should feel like cognitive overload, not like a narrator's convenient access to exposition. She reads surface thoughts, not deep motivations, and the thoughts she catches should be fragmentary, mundane, and sometimes misleading.

Prestige-creep: the prose drifting toward solemn literary register when the scene's natural mode is comic, warm, or chaotic. This novel's emotional power comes from the coexistence of comedy and genuine feeling, not from the comedy yielding to seriousness.

Briefing-itis: scenes where characters explain plot to each other or to the reader. Information should emerge through action, observation, and telepathic fragments — not through expository dialogue formatted as conversation.

Combat-poetry: fight scenes that become lyrical or aesthetically admiring. Violence should be fast, specific, physically grounded, and over quickly. Yor's lethality is matter-of-fact; Anya's fighting is scrappier and less elegant.

Emotion-labeling: telling the reader what a character feels instead of rendering the feeling through thought, action, and physical detail. 'She felt sad' is always worse than the specific thing that makes the sadness legible.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, primarily Anya. Chapter 7 (Damian), Chapter 10 (Yor), the Damian POV section in Chapter 13, and Damian's thread in Chapter 15 shift to close third on their respective POV characters. The telepathy layer is unique to Anya's POV — when she is the viewpoint character, other characters' surface thoughts appear as italicized interjections woven into the prose. In non-Anya POV chapters, no telepathic content appears. The Chapter 13 Damian POV section is structurally critical: it gives the reader access to Damian's interior at a depth Anya's telepathy cannot reach, and it reveals that he has identified Anya — knowledge the reader carries into the final act while Anya does not. This dramatic irony reversal is the novel's strongest structural surprise and depends on the reader experiencing Damian's depths unmediated by Anya's telepathic filter.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Action sequences: short sentences, concrete verbs, sensory immediacy. Comic scenes: longer sentences that build to turns, pacing that allows beats to land. Quiet domestic scenes: relaxed rhythm with room for observation and texture. Telepathic interjections should disrupt prose rhythm the way intrusive thoughts disrupt concentration — not ornamentally but as genuine cognitive interference. Avoid metronomic sentence patterns in any mode.
sensory_bias: visual surveillance — reading rooms, reading faces, the spy's hyperawareness of spatial arrangement and exit routes
auditory — overheard thoughts as a telepathic sixth sense layered over actual ambient sound
kinesthetic — action choreography, physical proximity, the body under stress or in intimacy
olfactory/gustatory — Yor's cooking, Berlint street food, the specific smell of institutional buildings and safe houses
